description: Plants and plant-like organisms found in ocean and coastal environments.
plants: Giant Kelp
A towering underwater forest swaying with the current, its fronds reaching from the seafloor to the surface.
Coral
A living reef built by tiny creatures over centuries, forming an underwater city of color and texture.
Sea Grass
Long green blades carpeting the sandy bottom, sheltering seahorses and small fish in their gentle sway.
Mangrove
A tree walking into the sea on stilted roots, its tangled legs trapping sediment and sheltering young fish.
Tide Pool Algae
Green and red films coating rocks exposed at low tide, slick underfoot and teeming with tiny life.
Dune Grass
Tough wiry stalks binding shifting sand together, holding the coastline in place through every storm.
Sargassum
Floating mats of golden-brown seaweed drifting on the open ocean, a world unto themselves.
Sea Lettuce
Thin translucent sheets of bright green waving in shallow water, delicate as wet paper.
Deep Sea Tube Worm Garden
Clusters of tall white tubes swaying near volcanic vents, thriving in absolute darkness on chemical heat.
Bioluminescent Plankton
Microscopic organisms that light up the water with blue-green fire when disturbed, turning waves into liquid light.
Salt Marsh Cordgrass
Dense stands of tough grass in brackish water, the quiet border between land and sea.
Coconut Palm
A tall palm leaning over the shore, dropping its heavy fruit into the surf to drift to new islands.
